frameworks.name: optimality
frameworks.one_line: statistical/Bayesian derivation of optimal solutions to perceptual problems

frameworks.name: heuristics
frameworks.one_line: simple approximations that achieve near-optimal perceptual goals without full computation

frameworks.name: ecological-computational-energetic constraints
frameworks.one_line: perceptual theory bounded by environment, computation cost, and metabolic budget

core_question: When is perceptual decision-making achieved by optimal computation versus by heuristic approximation?
key_assumptions: optimal solutions require perfect priors and complex computation
behavior may match optimal output without implementing optimal computation
ecological, computational, and energetic constraints shape mechanism
manipulations: cross-disciplinary review across perceptual decision tasks
key_findings: many perceptual behaviors can be approximated by heuristic strategies
optimality and heuristics can produce indistinguishable behavioral outputs
interpretations: searches for neural circuits implementing optimal computation will fail when behavior is actually heuristic
optimality should set computational goals while heuristics describe implementation under constraints
